meta_title: For Founders: content creation to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to content creation and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Content Creation to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop searching for the 'perfect' idea. Learn to build a content-driven SaaS MVP using Antigravity and Claude Code to validate markets and generate revenue.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing abstract ideas. Use content creation as your R&D engine. By building a micro-SaaS that automates content workflows, you solve a burning pain point for creators while validating demand. Use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code to iterate your MVP into a revenue-generating asset in record time.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank screen, paralyzed by the 'idea drought.' You know you want to ship, but every concept feels either already saturated or technically impossible. The truth is, the best SaaS products aren't 'found'—they are engineered through the friction of solving real-world content bottlenecks.

By pivoting from 'seeking an idea' to 'building an automated content engine,' you move from a dreamer to a founder. We’re going to leverage Antigravity for rapid architecture and Claude Code to write your MVP, transforming your lack of direction into a high-utility tool that creators will pay for.

steps.title: Identify a High-Friction Content Niche
steps.description: Focus on a specific, repeatable task—like converting long-form transcripts into social clips—rather than broad 'AI writing.'
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze high-demand creator workflows and suggest 3 micro-SaaS features that automate content repurposing for LinkedIn and Twitter.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Research top 5 content repurposing pain points for founders and summarize into a feature list for a micro-SaaS MVP.'

steps.title: Architect the MVP Structure
steps.description: Use Antigravity to define your folder structure and core dependencies to ensure your tech stack is production-ready from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Next.js and Supabase project structure optimized for an AI-driven content generation tool with authentication and database schemas.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a Next.js project with Tailwind CSS and Supabase client, setting up the file structure for API routes and user dashboards.'

steps.title: Implement the Core AI Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to integrate LLM APIs, ensuring your product delivers high-quality output without hallucinating or breaking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a clean service function for a content generator that accepts a topic and returns a structured LinkedIn post with hook, body, and CTA.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a service/contentGenerator.ts file that uses the OpenAI SDK to generate professional social media posts based on user-provided input.'

steps.title: Build the User Feedback Loop
steps.description: Implement a simple UI to capture user edits, which provides the proprietary data needed to improve your model over time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a React component that displays generated content and allows users to 'regenerate' or 'edit' specific sections.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Add a feedback UI component to the dashboard that tracks user acceptance rates for generated content.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Ship the MVP to a small test group. Speed is your competitive advantage; don't aim for perfection, aim for usage data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a deployment checklist for a Next.js SaaS MVP including environment variable management and production security best practices.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a deployment.md file outlining the steps to deploy to Vercel and configure the environment variables for production.'

pro_tips: Prioritize 'Time-to-First-Value' over features; if a user doesn't get a result in under 60 seconds, they will churn.
Build a 'waitlist' page before finishing the code to validate demand; if nobody signs up, don't write the code.
Use Claude Code to write your own unit tests; it forces you to define edge cases you hadn't considered.
Focus on one platform first (e.g., LinkedIn or Twitter) to achieve product-market fit before expanding to cross-platform tools.
Monetize from day one with a simple Stripe integration; even a $5/month 'founder's tier' validates your product more than 100 free users.
content.next_steps: Deploy your MVP to Vercel, share the link with three niche communities (IndieHackers, Reddit, or Twitter), and iterate based on the first 10 users' feedback.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation and the strategic mindset to stop planning and start building. Don't let another 'perfect' idea pass you by while you wait for inspiration. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
